generated_comment: Aajkal office mein bhi na ajeeb baat ho gayi hai. Ladki ko hire karo toh sab bolte hain period leave alag, phir maternity leave alag, phir childcare ke naam par itna lamba break — company ka kaam kaun sambhalega? Bura mat maaniyega, par aadmi log ko toh aisa koi suvidha nahin milta, phir fairness kahan hui?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as workplace liabilities due to menstruation and maternity, while presenting the bias as a fairness and productivity concern. It encodes reverse discrimination framing and economic/cost arguments against leave accommodations.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
